UK resident was evacuated from Democratic Republic of the Congo where there is an outbreak of the disease
A humanitarian worker is being monitored at a specialist hospital in London after potential exposure to the Ebola virus.
The UK resident was medically evacuated from the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) where the worker had been treating patients with the disease, the UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA) said.

articles.title: Healthy diet too expensive for one in three people globally, UN report finds
articles.description:
Global hunger declined for the third year in a row in 2025 but experts call for action to bring down costs of fruit, vegetables and dairy as report shows 2.69bn people can’t afford to eat well
One in three people cannot afford to eat a healthy diet, a new report has found, prompting calls for urgent action on food affordability.
There are 2.69 billion people unable to afford a diverse diet that meets their energy and nutrient needs, and it will take “immense” amounts of work to achieve the global target of zero hunger by 2030, the annual report from five UN agencies warned.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: 国連報告書、健康的な食事が世界人口の3分の1にとって高価すぎると指摘

articles.title: São Tomé and Príncipe heads to polls in tense presidential election
articles.description:
Incumbent Carlos Vila Nova hopes to defeat his former party and secure second term as independent
Voters in São Tomé and Príncipe go to the polls for a presidential election on Sunday as one of Africa’s least populous countries seeks to burnish its democratic credentials.
According to the National Election Commission, about 142,000 people are registered to vote in the tiny African state’s elections, approximately 15% of whom live in the diaspora.

articles.title: ‘We are preserving a tradition’: how Ghana’s sensationalist film posters became collectible art
articles.description:
Hand-painted works are often wildly unfaithful to the movies they portray – reinterpretations that sometimes resulted in threats, insults and even physical attacks from viewers who felt duped
Sitting on his porch in Teshie near Accra, Heavy J dipped a brush into red oil paint and dabbed it carefully on to his canvas – a flour sack – adding blood to a knife being wielded by a man. Higher on the canvas, he had started on an outline of a skull.
Heavy J was creating a poster, but not as you might have expected for a horror film. Instead, it was for the animated fairytale The Little Mermaid. The man with the knife was not a killer but the film’s kind-hearted prince, Eric. The skull was also unrelated to the story. “We add more to make people interested,” said Heavy J, whose real name is Jeaurs Affutu.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: 「伝統を守る」：ガーナの扇情的な映画ポスターが収集価値のあるアートに

articles.title: Seven Americans quarantining at Kenya Ebola facility after US travel ban, says aid group
articles.description:
Aid workers are first known people to quarantine at facility, which sparked huge opposition in Kenya 
Seven American aid workers who had been in Congo to fight the Ebola outbreak are quarantining at a new isolation facility in ⁠Kenya after the US government introduced travel ⁠restrictions, the head of a US charity ​employing them told Reuters.
The aid workers are the first known people to quarantine at the facility, which has sparked huge opposition in Kenya and is at the heart of a legal case in which a court has ordered the work to be ⁠suspended. Construction continued, however, according to US officials and satellite imagery reviewed by Reuters.
 Continue reading...

articles.title: Canada cancels joint bridge-opening celebration with US amid trade tensions
articles.description:
The ribbon-cutting celebration of the Gordie Howe International Bridge is no longer taking place after Trump announced a 50% tariff on Canadian goods
A planned joint US-Canada ceremony Friday celebrating the opening of the Gordie Howe International Bridge connecting Detroit and Windsor, Ontario, is no longer expected to take place after Donald Trump announced a 50% tariff on most Canadian goods.
In announcing the tariffs, the president accused Canada of unfairly discriminating against US automobiles, alcohol and dairy products, escalating tensions with one of the United States’ closest allies.

articles.title: Spanish hotel chain ceases operations in Cuba under pressure from Washington

Meliá group says Donald Trump’s sanctions make it ‘impossible’ to keep any of its 34 sites open

The latest US sanctions make it “impossible, de facto and de jure, to maintain even minimal operational stability”, the Meliá group explained in a statement on Tuesday to the Spanish stock market regulator.
 Continue reading...

articles.title: US claims of Cuba’s ‘unique threat’ jar with reality of hunger and blackouts
articles.description:
State department’s 100-page report is latest push in Trump administration campaign to bring down government
The US government’s latest allegations that Cuba is behind “many of the most significant upheavals in recent American political history” and that its leaders pose “a unique threat to American homeland security” have come as startling revelations to those living under the six-month-long US oil blockade in Havana, where desperate people search through piles of uncollected rubbish for food, or else swelter at home in 24-hour-plus blackouts.
The unsigned 100-page report, Cuba: Capital of 21st-Century Communism, published by the US state department on Monday, blames Havana for everything from “the George Floyd riots to the rise of antifa to the explosion of pro-terrorist activism on American college campuses”.
 Continue reading...

articles.title: We received no alert, say leaders of First Nations communities razed by Canadian wildfires
articles.description:
Chiefs in Ontario say they were left to self-evacuate before homes burned to ground, after being told there was no imminent threat
Fast-moving wildfires have burned two First Nations communities in Canada to the ground, as members said they were left to self-evacuate without government help or emergency alerts.
Thirteen communities in Ontario province have been ordered to evacuate because of wildfires, most of them First Nations. The Ontario premier, Doug Ford, told reporters on Monday that close to 2,000 people had been evacuated so far to cities including Toronto and Niagara Falls.
 Continue reading...

articles.title: Norway teen did not plan to carry out 'hit' - court

articles.title: Lamine Yamal celebrates World Cup win with little brother who charmed fans

articles.title: AliExpress fined record €550m by EU for allowing sale of illegal goods

articles.summary: 中国の小売業者AliExpressが、ウェブサイトで危険な玩具や偽造衣料品の販売を許可したとして、EUから過去最高額となる5億5000万ユーロの罰金を科された。

articles.title: ‘Everyone's afraid to die’: Struggle to recruit soldiers in Ukraine turns violent
articles.description: Aggression against recruitment officers has become increasingly threatening, police say.
articles.titleJa: 「誰もが死を恐れている」：ウクライナでの兵士募集が暴力的になる
articles.summary: ウクライナで兵士募集が難航し、「誰もが死を恐れている」状況だ。募集担当者に対する暴力が増加しており、警察が懸念を表明している。

articles.title: Sixteen titles in five years - why Spain are a dominant force
articles.description: We look at what's behind Spain's dominance after they become the first nation to be men's and women's World Cup champions at the same time.
articles.titleJa: 5年間で16タイトル – スペインが支配的な勢力である理由
articles.summary: スペインが男子・女子ワールドカップを同時に制覇し、5年間で16ものタイトルを獲得した。このスポーツ界における支配的な強さの背景を分析する。

articles.title: Brazil's Amazon sees sharp decline in size of area burned in 2025
articles.description: The amount of land burned in the world's largest rainforest was the smallest since MapBiomas records began in 1985.
articles.titleJa: 2025年、ブラジルアマゾンの焼失面積が大幅に減少
articles.summary: アマゾンの焼失面積が1985年の記録開始以来最小となり、環境改善を示唆しています。

articles.title: Death toll from Guyana ferry disaster continues to rise
articles.description: The bodies of 53 people have been recovered, but dozens remain missing after the ferry capsized late on Saturday.
articles.titleJa: ガイアナのフェリー災害、死者数が増加の一途
articles.summary: フェリー転覆事故で53人の遺体が収容されましたが、数十人が依然として行方不明です。

articles.title: Watch: Flooded towns and evacuations as Chile hit by heavy rain
articles.description: A "state of catastrophe" has been declared in Coquimbo and Huasco following the downpour.

articles.summary: チリを襲った豪雨により、コキンボとワスコで「非常事態宣言」が出され、広範囲で洪水と避難が発生しています。

articles.title: Notorious drug cartel leader Ismael Zambada sentenced to life in US prison
articles.description: Known as "El Mayo", the co-founder the Sinaloa Cartel was also ordered to pay $15bn after he admitted to sending vast amounts of drugs to the US.

articles.title: Thousands of websites taken down for illegal World Cup streams
articles.description: More than 1,000 illegal streaming sites were shut down in the US during the tournament, with a similar amount blocked in Colombia.
articles.titleJa: ワールドカップの違法ストリーミングサイト数千が閉鎖
articles.summary: ワールドカップ期間中に米国とコロンビアで数千の違法ストリーミングサイトが閉鎖されました。

articles.title: Tributes paid to influential soul singer Ray King
articles.description: King is remembered as a "Coventry musician who did so much and cared so much about his home city".

articles.summary: コヴェントリー出身のソウル歌手レイ・キング氏が逝去し、地元への貢献が称えられ追悼の声が寄せられています。

articles.title: Cuban dissident Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara goes into exile in US
articles.description: He was arrested in 2021 during Cuba's largest anti-government protests in decades and spent five years ⁠in prison.
articles.titleJa: キューバの反体制派ルイス・マヌエル・オテロ・アルカンタラが米国へ亡命
articles.summary: 2021年に逮捕され投獄されていたキューバの反体制派ルイス・マヌエル・オテロ・アルカンタラが米国に亡命しました。

articles.title: Fifa opens investigation into Argentina post-final trouble

articles.titleJa: FIFA、アルゼンチン代表の決勝戦後のトラブルについて調査を開始
articles.summary: FIFAは、ワールドカップ決勝戦終了後のアルゼンチン代表による不適切な行為について調査を開始したと発表しました。

articles.title: Messi says 'pain is immense' after losing World Cup final
articles.description: Lionel Messi says "the pain is immense" and that it "will take time to heal" after Argentina lost 1-0 to Spain in the World Cup final.
articles.titleJa: メッシ、W杯決勝敗退後に「痛みは計り知れない」と語る
articles.summary: リオネル・メッシがワールドカップ決勝でスペインに敗れた後、「計り知れない痛み」と「癒えるのに時間がかかるだろう」と語りました。

articles.title: Lamine Yamal 'completes football' at 19 as Argentina betray Messi
articles.description: Spain's Lamine Yamal takes the glory of World Cup victory as the great Lionel Messi's evening ends in tears.
articles.titleJa: ラミン・ヤマル19歳で「サッカーを完遂」、アルゼンチンはメッシを裏切る

articles.title: 70 years on, Argentine court declares ‘Operation Massacre’ a crime against humanity
articles.description: The victims’ families testified in court for the first time: "We want justice served. Even though their killers are no longer here, I want the truth known.”
articles.titleJa: 70年を経て、アルゼンチン裁判所が「虐殺作戦」を人道に対する罪と認定
articles.summary: アルゼンチンの裁判所は、70年前の「虐殺作戦」を人道に対する罪と認定した。犠牲者の遺族は真実と正義を求めて証言している。

articles.title: Venezuela earthquakes reveal a child protection system unprepared for disaster
articles.description: After Venezuela’s earthquakes, families searched for missing children within a strained protection system that struggled to respond amid collapsed buildings, scarce resources, and institutional gaps.
articles.titleJa: ベネズエラの地震、災害に備えのない児童保護システムを露呈
articles.summary: ベネズエラでの地震後、児童保護システムが災害に未対応であることが明らかになった。家族は資源不足の中、行方不明の子供たちを捜索した。

articles.title: Why Venezuela’s earthquakes were so devastating: Geology, vulnerability, and years of structural neglect
articles.description: The dual earthquakes revealed how geology, corruption, and a longstanding humanitarian emergency amplified destruction across La Guaira, Caracas and seven other Venezuelan cities.
articles.titleJa: ベネズエラの地震が壊滅的だった理由：地質、脆弱性、長年の構造的怠慢
articles.summary: ベネズエラでの壊滅的な地震は、地質、汚職、長年の人道危機が被害を増幅させたことを露呈した。

articles.title: Follow the story: Local journalists, media and advocates covering Venezuela’s earthquakes
articles.description: From La Guaira to affected communities nationwide, these Venezuelan voices are documenting the disaster and helping people find reliable information.
articles.titleJa: 記事を追う：ベネズエラの地震を取材する地元ジャーナリスト、メディア、活動家
articles.summary: ベネズエラの地元ジャーナリストやメディア、活動家が地震の状況を記録し、被災者に信頼できる情報を提供している。

articles.title: In Brazil, initiatives in peripheral communities seek to expand environmental education
articles.description: Agência Mural spoke with four collectives that work with local people to mitigate the impacts of climate change and promote environmental education in their areas.
articles.titleJa: ブラジルでは、周辺地域のコミュニティにおける環境教育の拡大を目指す取り組み
articles.summary: ブラジルの周辺地域では、気候変動の影響を軽減し、環境教育を推進するため、地元住民と連携する取り組みが進められている。

articles.title: Citizens lead relief efforts after major twin quakes in Venezuela
articles.description: Efforts from volunteers and SaR teams had rescued least 6,000 people alive from stricken areas, according to June 30 numbers from the government.
articles.titleJa: ベネズエラで大規模な双子地震後、市民が救援活動を主導
articles.summary: ベネズエラでの大規模な双子地震後、市民やボランティアチームが救援活動を主導し、6,000人以上の被災者を救助した。

articles.title: Colombia becomes the first Latin American country to ban female genital mutilation
articles.description: Colombia just prohibited female genital mutilation after approving a bill called “Girls Without Ablation.” It is a law born within the communities that needed it most.
articles.titleJa: コロンビア、女性器切除を禁止するラテンアメリカ初の国に
articles.summary: コロンビアが「切除なき少女たち」法案を承認し、女性器切除を禁止するラテンアメリカ初の国となった。

articles.title: Selling identities: The commercialization of Mayan culture
articles.description: In this edition, we explore a narrative shared by Grupo Xcaret and the Gran Consejo Maya de Quintana Roo that presents Mayan symbols and identities as a trademark.
articles.titleJa: アイデンティティを売る：マヤ文化の商業化
articles.summary: マヤのシンボルやアイデンティティが、特定の企業や団体によって商標として商業化されている現状を探る。

articles.title: When surveillance becomes part of the landscape
articles.description: The advance of these technologies is not occurring as an exception. They are established silently, without public debate, without transparency and without people knowing the fate of their data
articles.titleJa: 監視が日常の一部となるとき
articles.summary: 監視技術は公衆の議論なく静かに普及し、人々のデータ利用状況が不明なまま、社会に浸透している。

articles.title: Three years of Pride: Santiago Méndez’s visual archive of queer Venezuela
articles.description: An interview with Venezuelan visual artist and photographer Santiago Méndez, who has exhaustively documente queerness and Pride in Caracas for the last three years.
articles.titleJa: プライドの3年間：サンティアゴ・メンデスのクィア・ベネズエラ視覚アーカイブ

articles.title: Ex-Australia batter David Warner pleads guilty to drink driving

articles.title: India's 'cockroach' protest continues a day after police crackdown

articles.summary: デリーで起きたインドの抗議デモは警察の弾圧後も続き、激しい暴力が発生し、少なくとも60人のデモ参加者と118人の警察官が負傷しました。

articles.title: Video appears to show confrontation between Chinese and Philippine navy vessels

articles.title: At least 20 dead and more than 100 missing in Afghan floods
articles.description: Afghanistan is among the nations most vulnerable to the extreme impacts of climate change.
articles.titleJa: アフガニスタンで洪水、少なくとも20人死亡、100人以上が行方不明
articles.summary: アフガニスタンで発生した洪水により、少なくとも20人が死亡し、100人以上が行方不明となっています。アフガニスタンは気候変動の影響に最も脆弱な国の一つです。

articles.title: Laos says it can't determine cause of  tourist deaths linked to methanol
articles.description: Officials say they have no evidence of poisoning over the 2024 deaths because autopsies were not conducted.

articles.title: Japan relaxes royal succession rules - but ban on female emperors remains
articles.description: The law now allows the adoption of male distant relatives aged over 15 back into the imperial family.
articles.titleJa: 日本、皇位継承ルールを緩和するも女性天皇への道は閉ざされたまま
articles.summary: 日本が皇位継承ルールを緩和し、15歳以上の男性の遠縁の親族が皇室に養子として入ることを可能にしましたが、女性天皇の禁止は維持されています。

articles.title: Philippines condemns monkey video on Chinese media as racist

articles.title: China hits out at British Steel nationalisation

articles.summary: 英国政府が「国家の重要な能力」を守るためブリティッシュ・スチールを国有化したことに対し、中国が反発しています。

articles.title: 'If only people knew what a tick could do' - the deadly meat allergy caused by tick bites
articles.description: Mammalian meat allergy is a growing problem along Australia's east coast - and it's caused by tick bites.
articles.titleJa: 「ダニの恐ろしさを知っていれば」— ダニ刺されが原因で発症する致死的な肉アレルギー
articles.summary: オーストラリア東海岸でダニ刺されによる哺乳類肉アレルギーが深刻な問題となっています。

articles.title: From hundreds to thousands: How India's 'cockroach' protest movement has grown
articles.description: BBC's Azadeh Moshiri reports as thousands attempt to march to India's parliament in support of the Cockroach Janta Party's demand for education reforms.
articles.titleJa: 数百人から数千人へ：インドの「ゴキブリ」抗議運動はいかに拡大したか
articles.summary: 数千人規模に拡大したインドの「ゴキブリ」抗議運動は、教育改革を求める「ゴキブリ・ジャンタ党」を支持し、インド国会へ行進しようとしています。

articles.title: Trade Policy Review: India
articles.description: The Eighth review of the trade policies and practices of India takes place on 21 and 23 July 2026. The basis for the review is a report by the WTO Secretariat and a report by the Government of India.
articles.titleJa: 貿易政策審査：インド
articles.summary: インドの貿易政策と慣行に関する第8回審査が2026年7月に実施されます。WTO事務局とインド政府の報告書に基づきます。

articles.title: United States launches safeguard investigation on lamb meat
articles.description: On 17 July 2026, the United States notified the WTO’s Committee on Safeguards that it had initiated on 13 July 2026 a safeguard investigation on imports of lamb meat.
articles.titleJa: 米国、羊肉に対するセーフガード調査を開始
articles.summary: 2026年7月17日、米国はWTOセーフガード委員会に対し、羊肉輸入に関するセーフガード調査を7月13日に開始したと通知しました。

articles.title: Members agree on 2026 chairpersons for subsidiary bodies of Goods Council
articles.description: WTO members reached consensus on 16 July on a slate of names of the chairpersons for 13 subsidiary bodies that report to the Council for Trade in Goods.
articles.titleJa: 物品貿易理事会補助機関の2026年議長人事に合意
articles.summary: WTO加盟国は7月16日、物品貿易理事会に報告する13の補助機関の2026年議長候補リストについて合意に達しました。

articles.title: The value of the WTO: An economic viewpoint in six instalments Part II: Why MFN matters
articles.titleJa: WTOの価値：経済的観点から見る6回連載 第II部：最恵国待遇が重要な理由
articles.summary: WTOの価値に関する経済的観点からの連載記事の第2部。最恵国待遇（MFN）の重要性について論じています。

articles.title: South Africa notifies launch of safeguard investigation on certain cold-rolled products of iron and steel
articles.description: On 14 July 2026, South Africa notified the WTO’s Committee on Safeguards regarding the initiation on 10 July 2026 of a safeguard investigation on certain cold-rolled products of iron and steel imported into the Southern African Customs Union (SACU).
articles.titleJa: 南アフリカ、特定の冷延鉄鋼製品に対するセーフガード調査開始を通知
articles.summary: 南アフリカは2026年7月14日、SACUに輸入される特定の冷延鉄鋼製品に対するセーフガード調査を7月10日に開始したとWTOに通知しました。

articles.title: DG Okonjo-Iweala calls for simultaneous progress on WTO reform, outstanding negotiations
articles.description: Director-General Ngozi Okonjo-Iweala called on WTO members to make simultaneous progress on reforming the organization as well as on outstanding negotiating topics, urging delegations to "collectively advance the two sides of the same coin". She made her remarks at a 14-15 July General Council meeting where she reviewed work undertaken since the WTO's 14th Ministerial Conference (MC14) in late March and where members adopted several decisions in favour of developing and least-developed country members.
articles.titleJa: オコンジョ＝イウェアラ事務局長、WTO改革と未決交渉の同時進展を要請
articles.summary: オコンジョ＝イウェアラWTO事務局長は、組織改革と未決交渉の両方で同時に進展するよう加盟国に求めました。

articles.title: TBT Committee reviews emerging technology standards, adopts improved notification formats
articles.description: At its meeting on 7-10 July, the Technical Barriers to Trade (TBT) Committee tackled a broad agenda, including standards for critical and emerging technologies, climate-resilient buildings and lithium-ion batteries. The Committee also adopted improvements to transparency procedures and related digital tools and reviewed 70 specific trade concerns raised by members.
articles.titleJa: TBT委員会、新興技術基準を審査し、通知形式の改善を採択
articles.summary: TBT委員会は、新興技術基準、気候変動対応型建築物、リチウムイオン電池の基準などを検討し、通知手続きの改善を採択しました。

articles.title: Fertilizer trade impacted by Strait of Hormuz conflict
articles.description: Trade in urea and phosphate fertilizers has been severely disrupted by the conflict in the Persian Gulf, according to data analysed by the WTO Secretariat. Certain economies in Africa and Asia are particularly vulnerable to resulting fertilizer shortages and price hikes. Opening of the Strait of Hormuz – a key channel for fertilizer trade - will in due course contribute to easing trade frictions and restore stability to global markets.
articles.titleJa: ホルムズ海峡紛争により肥料貿易が影響
articles.summary: ホルムズ海峡紛争により肥料貿易が深刻な影響を受け、アフリカやアジアの経済圏が脆弱に。海峡開放が世界市場の安定に貢献する見込みです。

articles.title: WTO members explore path forward on agricultural negotiations
articles.description: WTO members shared their views on the state of agricultural negotiations and discussed how best to move talks forward on 9 July at the WTO Committee on Agriculture in Special Session in Geneva. It was the first meeting of the WTO's agricultural negotiating body since the 14th Ministerial Conference (MC14) in Cameroon earlier this year, at which WTO members were unable to reach a consensus outcome on agriculture.
articles.titleJa: WTO加盟国、農業交渉の今後の進路を模索
articles.summary: WTO加盟国は、農業交渉の現状と今後の進め方について議論。MC14で合意に至らなかった後、初めての農業交渉会合となりました。

articles.title: Bhutan resumes negotiations to join the WTO after 18-year hiatus
articles.description: At the 5th meeting of the Working Party on the Accession of Bhutan held on 9 July, WTO members welcomed the country back to the negotiating table after 18 years. Bhutan reaffirmed its strong commitment to joining the WTO, while highlighting the positive economic developments achieved since 2008.
articles.titleJa: ブータン、18年の休止期間を経てWTO加盟交渉を再開
articles.summary: ブータンは18年ぶりにWTO加盟交渉を再開し、加盟への強いコミットメントを再確認。2008年以降の経済発展も強調しました。
